title: NAAMSE: Evolutionary Security Evaluation Framework for Agents
description: Introduces NAAMSE, an evolutionary AI red-teaming framework and fuzzer designed to test LLM agents for security and utility. The framework utilizes genetic algorithms for real-time attack mutation, balancing Attack Success Rate (ASR) and False Positive Rate (FPR) to mitigate the 'over-blocking' problem in agentic systems. Recognized with 2nd place at the Berkeley RDI AgentX competition.

title: HASHIRU: Hierarchical Agent System for Hybrid Intelligent Resource Utilization
description: Rapid Large Language Model (LLM) advancements are fueling autonomous Multi-Agent System (MAS) development. However, current frameworks often lack flexibility, resource awareness, model diversity, and autonomous tool creation. This paper introduces HASHIRU (Hierarchical Agent System for Hybrid Intelligent Resource Utilization), a novel MAS framework featuring a 'CEO' agent dynamically managing specialized 'employee' agents. Evaluations demonstrate HASHIRU's capabilities in complex reasoning, outperforming Gemini 2.0 Flash on benchmarks like GSM8K (96% vs. 61%) and JEEBench (80% vs. 68.3%).

title: gem5 Vision
description: The gem5 Vision Project seeks to improve user-friendliness and accessibility by introducing advanced search functionality, comprehensive resource categorization, and expanded database support within the gem5 ecosystem for researchers and developers.
